title: Growing an Overnight Bacteria Culture
author: Katy M Monteith
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vbne2me

steps.instruction: Pour appropriate volume of LB broth into your selected autoclaved conical container. For efficient bacterial growth in media, the media should not come above the widest area of the conical shaped flask/tube. Therefore, it is important to chose an appropriate sized flask/tube depending on how much media you plan to inoculate. For a 10 mL inoculation, a 50 mL falcon tube is recommended.

Open and add the frozen aliquot of bacteria to the LB broth OR using an inoculation loop, carefully select your isolated colony from the media plate and shake the loop in media until the fragment has come loose and is visibly floating in media.
Loosely seal the container, either using tape to secure the loosely fitted lid of a tube or tinfoil to cover a flask

Place the tube/flask in an orbital shaker at ~140rpm at the optimal growing temperature of your bacterial species (this will likely be 30 or 37° C). Leave the liquid culture to grow overnight for approximately 12-14 hrs. Before the bacteria culture is used it is prudent to check the optical density using an Absorbance Microplate reader. If using the bacteria directly then it is important to assure the culture is in the exponential growth phase (OD600 ~0.6-0.8). If reinoculating the bacteria into a larger volume of LB broth the growth phase of the bacteria is less important but if reinoculated during the lag or stationary phase of growth then the reinoculated culture will take longer to reach the exponential growth phase.
